The Origin Story

Over 50 Years of
Sinking George

When we opened the Barnard General Store in 2013, one of the first things we were told was that we absolutely had to keep up the Guess George's Fate Contest. It's one of those Barnard traditions that just keeps going — but for years, nobody could tell us exactly how it started.

That changed when our Town Manager, Kassie Hull, stumbled upon an old issue of Vermont Life magazine from Spring 1983. Tucked inside was a feature called "Sinking George" — and it told the whole story.

In 1974, the directors of Barnard Academy's kindergarten needed to raise money for supplies. Their solution was inspired: stuff a man, set him up in a chair on Silver Lake directly across from the General Store, and sell tickets to anyone willing to guess the date and time he'd fall through the ice. They called him George. Half the proceeds went to the winner, and half went to the school. The idea, as the article put it, worked beautifully.

George went on to appear every spring after that — except 1981, when there weren't enough children enrolled to hold kindergarten. Residents were disappointed, and when he came back the following year, he was eagerly welcomed. Clothes and stuffing were donated by townspeople, including hay from a local farm and an orange hunting cap to protect George's head. A rope was tied from George all the way to the town flagpole, so he could be reeled in after his dunking.

The kindergarten children felt close to George. They walked past the General Store and Silver Lake every week on their way to the library, and they always stopped to say hello.

We decided it was time to take the contest back to its roots. This year, Principal Zoerheide and the Fun Friday Group at Barnard Academy built a brand new George — and he's out on the ice where he belongs. Going forward, this is how it will always be done: the kids make George. Just like 1974.

Half of every dollar raised goes to the winner. The other half goes directly to Barnard Academy.

Vermont Life Magazine — Spring 1983
"Sinking George" — the original article, Vermont Life 1983
Barnard kindergartners watching George on Silver Lake, circa 1983

Contest Rules

Guess the exact date and time George falls through the ice on Silver Lake.
The closest guess wins. Ties are broken by order of entry.
Payment via PayPal must be completed to confirm your entry.
All entries are final. No refunds.
The winner receives 50% of contest proceeds. The other 50% goes to Barnard Academy.
Winner is notified by email and announced at Barnard General Store.
